memoryArtifacts.headline: Gravestone of John Baker Tremaine Parish Church Cornwall
memoryArtifacts.description:
The transcription of the headstone reads
"Here lieth the Body of John
Baker of this Parish who was
Buried the 29th day of July
1793 in the 63rd year of his age"

sourceList.title: John Baker in entry for Mary Baker, "England Births and Christenings, 1538-1975"
Burial of John Baker 29 July 1793 Tremaine Parish Register Cornwall

zh.Jacobstow, Cornwall, England: 英國可瓦爾亞可布斯托福
zh.Tremaine Churchyard, Tremaine, Cornwall,: 英國英格蘭可瓦爾特曼
zh.lifeSummary: 當John Le Baker III於1130年4月19日出生在英國可瓦爾亞可布斯托福時，他的父親John Le Baker II20歲，母親Mary Buber16歲。他於大約1159年與Margery Hambley在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1193年7月在英國可瓦爾特曼享年63歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭可瓦爾特曼。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Le Baker III于1130年4月19日出生在英国可瓦爾亞可布斯托福时，他的父亲John Le Baker II20岁，母亲Mary Buber16岁。他于大约1159年与Margery Hambley在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1193年7月在英国可瓦爾特曼享年63岁，埋葬在英国英格兰可瓦爾特曼。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Baker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name, from Middle English bakere, Old English bæcere, a derivative of bacan ‘to bake’. It may have been used for someone whose special task in the kitchen of a great house or castle was the baking of bread, but since most humbler households did their own baking in the Middle Ages, it may also have referred to the owner of a communal oven used by the whole village. The right to be in charge of this and exact money or loaves in return for its use was in many parts of the country a hereditary feudal privilege. Compare Miller . Less often the surname may have been acquired by someone noted for baking particularly fine bread or by a baker of pottery or bricks.

Americanized form (translation into English) of surnames meaning ‘baker’, for example Dutch Bakker , German Becker and Beck , French Boulanger and Bélanger (see Belanger ), Czech Pekař, Slovak Pekár, and Croatian Pekar .

History: Baker was established as an early immigrant surname in Puritan New England. Among others, two men called Remember Baker (father and son) lived at Woodbury, CT, in the early 17th century, and an Alexander Baker arrived in Boston, MA, in 1635.

variants.name: Miller
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a miller. The standard modern vocabulary word represents the northern Middle English term miller, an agent derivative of mille ‘mill’, reinforced by Old Norse mylnari (see Milner ). In southern, western, and central England Millward (literally, ‘mill keeper’) was the usual term. In North America, the surname Miller has absorbed many cognate surnames from other languages, for example German Müller (see Mueller ), Dutch Mulder and Molenaar , French Meunier , Italian Molinaro , Spanish Molinero , Hungarian Molnár (see Molnar ), Slovenian, Croatian, and Serbian Mlinar , Polish Młynarz or Młynarczyk (see Mlynarczyk ). Miller (including in the senses below) is the seventh most frequent surname in the US.

South German, Swiss German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Müller ‘miller’ (see Mueller ) and, in North America, also an altered form of this. This form of the surname is also found in other European countries, notably in Poland, Denmark, France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), and Czechia; compare 3 below.
Americanized form of Polish, Czech, Croatian, Serbian, and Slovenian   Miler   ‘miller’, a surname of German origin.

variants.name: Baxter
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English baxter ‘baker’ (from Old English bæcestre ‘baker’, earlier ‘female baker’, the feminine equivalent of bæcere). Compare Baker .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When John Le Baker III was born on 19 April 1130, in Jacobstow, Cornwall, England, his father, John Le Baker II, was 20 and his mother, Mary Buber, was 16. He married Margery Hambley about 1159, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in July 1193, in Tremaine, Cornwall, England, at the age of 63, and was buried in Tremaine, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Le Baker III est né le 19 avril 1130, à Jacobstow, Cornouailles, Angleterre, son père, John Le Baker II, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Mary Buber, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Margery Hambley environ 1159, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en juillet 1193, à Tremaine, Cornouailles, Angleterre, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Tremaine, Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Le Baker III am 19. April 1130 in Jacobstow, Cornwall, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Le Baker II 20 und seine Mutter Mary Buber 16. Er hat um 1159 in England Margery Hambley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb im Juli 1193 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Tremaine, Cornwall, England und wurde in Tremaine, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Le Baker III è nato il il 19 aprile 1130 nel Jacobstow, Cornwall, England, il suo padre, John Le Baker II, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Mary Buber, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Margery Hambley circa 1159, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel luglio 1193, nel Tremaine, Cornwall, England, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Tremaine, Cornwall, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: John Le Baker IIIが1130年4月19日にイギリスコルンワルジャコブストウで生まれた時，父John Le Baker IIは20歳，母Mary Buberは16歳であった。約1159年にイギリスでMargery Hambleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1193年7月にイギリスコルンワルトレメインで63歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスコルンワルトレメインにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Le Baker III(이)가 1130년 4월 19일 영국 코른월 재컵스타우에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Le Baker II(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Buber(은)는 16세였다. 그는 대략 1159년 영국에서 Margery Hambley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1193년 7월 영국 코른월 트렘언에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 트렘언에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Le Baker III nasceu em 19 de abril de 1130, em Jacobstow, Cornwall, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Le Baker II, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Mary Buber, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Margery Hambley aproximadamente 1159, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em julho de 1193, em Tremaine, Cornwall, Inglaterra, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Tremaine, Cornwall, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Le Baker III родился 19 апреля 1130 г. в местности джакобстоу, корнвалл, Англия, его отцу по имени John Le Baker II было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Buber, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Margery Hambley около 1159 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер июль 1193 г. в местности тремеин, корнвалл, Англия в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности тремеин, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Le Baker III nació el 19 de abril de 1130, en Jacobstow, Cornwall, Inglaterra, su papá, John Le Baker II, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Mary Buber, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Margery Hambley aproximadamente 1159, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en julio de 1193, en Tremaine, Cornwall, Inglaterra, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Tremaine, Cornwall, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
